Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class ReportBucketField

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.ReportBucketField
public class ReportBucketField extends Object

Java class for ReportBucketField complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="ReportBucketField">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="bucketType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ReportBucketFieldType"/>
         <element name="developerName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="nullTreatment" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ReportFormulaNullTreatment" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="otherBucketLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sourceColumnName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="useOther" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="values" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ReportBucketFieldValue" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getValues

      public List<ReportBucketFieldValue> getValues()
      Gets the value of the values property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the values property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getValues().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ReportBucketFieldValue

      Returns:
      The value of the values property.
